summary: Europe's largest country by area (excluding Russia), at war with Russia since 2014 (full-scale invasion 2022), with strategic importance as the breadbasket of Europe and a front line in the contest between NATO and Russia.

history.detail: Medieval Slavic state centred on Kyiv; cultural ancestor of Ukraine, Russia and Belarus.
Cossack Hetmanate enters alliance with Russian Tsar — disputed historical meaning.
Brief independence during Russian Revolution; crushed by Bolsheviks.
Soviet-engineered famine kills 3.5–7M Ukrainians; recognised as genocide by many countries.
Ukraine suffered worst civilian casualties in Europe during WWII.
Nuclear reactor explosion; largest nuclear accident in history.
Ukraine votes to leave USSR; nuclear weapons relinquished under Budapest Memorandum 1994.
Pro-EU uprising; president flees; Russia annexes Crimea, war in Donbas begins.
Russia invades Feb 24; massive Western military and financial support begins.

finance.currency_role: Hryvnia (UAH); heavily supported by IMF and G7 loans

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Pre-war: growing IT outsourcing hub; war disrupted but remote work continues

resources.oil_gas: Some gas; heavily dependent on imports
resources.rare_earths: Significant titanium, lithium, manganese, neon gas (critical for chip manufacturing)
resources.water: Dnieper River basin; Crimea water conflict
resources.agriculture: World's largest sunflower oil exporter; major wheat and corn producer

follow_the_money.detail: Over $100B from US, EU, UK since 2022
Billions in emergency financing; future EU reconstruction funds
Black Sea grain deal (suspended 2023); major food supply leverage

theories_disputed.detail: JIT confirmed Russian BUK missile (53rd Brigade) shot down MH17 over Donbas 2014.
Russia claimed US biolabs in Ukraine; WHO and US deny offensive programs; defensive research labs confirmed.

connections.detail: Largest single donor of military aid
Major refugee host and weapons transit route
